reviews.review_description: I like the premise of this game, though definitely feels like a bad hallucination (reminds me of when I had Australian Flu, not good!) What I'm really struggling with though is the motion sickness, I don't get it with any other games I play (apart from Epic Rollercoaster, which is kind of to be expected) so I can only play for a little while. Which is a bit disappointing...

developer_response.body: Thank you so much for your feedback! To reduce motion sickness, try playing in teleport mode (instantaneous beam movement) or in Roomscale mode.

reviews.review_description:
I like the game, but somewhere around room 10, the pill bottles no longer show up in my inventory, and I’m unable to solve the second room in part 2 so Am I missing something?

UPDATE: Developer has explained that the pills are limited, and that all except level one can be completed without them.

reviews.review_title: Don't play till they add a save progress
reviews.review_description: Dissapointing to have to replay entire game just to see next room apparently the game has absolutely no saves,  no progress saves at all, no continue after completing first 14 rms , u can only start over. I'd wait till its completed to play over again.

This was amazing! The graphics, the story (creepy, but not scary), and excellent physics. I had no glitches and no issues. Movement, crouching, puzzle physics worked great. Such a different and fun dynamic. The graphics were amazing. Of course, it was on the short side, but definitely looking forward to the next chapter. Great work!
Update: 2 new “rooms” that continue the story and are so good! Can’t wait for more
Keeps getting better! Now trying to figure out the new lab room with the different Belinda costumes.
Another update: yay, so happy to get into the train cab and blow the horn! Fun puzzles.

Wow, Loved it, weird without being too scary, puzzles not too hard so gives good sense of achievement and keeps the experience moving along. Graphics are fabulous, although a bit dark at times, as in lighting, its dark themes all the way in the story !!! only 1 annoyance was the options handheld thing, what a faff ! Overall its brilliant and I'm looking forward to the next installment,

Update 25-4-2026

What a great addition the last room is. Very nicely made. The puzzles in the train cabin are a bit more challenging but at the same time impressively done. The reward when you finish and the train starts moving. Wow. Super cool. The only comment is that if another room is added, I have to go through everything all over again from the beginning every time. Fortunately, all the rooms are worth it.

Update 11-8-2025

I just played level 12. It's also very well-designed. The puzzles aren't too difficult, unfortunately. But the environment and sound make up for it.

It's very well-designed. The atmosphere is well-created. The sounds and gameplay are a good match. The image quality isn't great, but that doesn't make it any less fun. The puzzles are well-designed. Enjoy playing the game.

Loved  this game 
And so cheap.  If you like puzzles get this you will teally enjoy it

reviews.review_title: One for the older puzzle game fans.
reviews.review_description:
Darktrip is an trippy ride through the minds of the games creator.
What starts out as a relatively normal escape room games just becomes weirder and weirder!
That's without ingesting the drugs!

Unlike many escape room games where you will play once and then move on the game encourages you to experience it multiple times.

Darktrip has a mechanic where you can choose to tackle puzzles whilst drugged up (complete with trippy visual effects) or sober.

Take your pick.....how do you like it?
Weird or weird? 


Worth picking up for puzzle fans and for those looking for a game that deals with the occult or the macabre.

I haven't been able to get the game to work correctly on Quest 3 yet (it's too dark and the controls are not working right), but it works fine on my Quest 2. On both devices I had to clear the app data in settings to get the TV start button to work. After that it was smooth sailing on the Quest 2, and I was able to make it through to the current ending. It did take me a few minutes to get the hang of changing the inventory on the wrist band, and it might be a good addition to put this into the tutorial at the beginning. Once the developers get the bugs worked out, this will definitely be a 5-star game, especially for those who like escape room experiences and don't mind some Lovecraftian horror with adult themes. They do a great job of combining storytelling, audio/visual elements, and challenges that are not too difficult.

developer_response.body: Thank you so much for this feedback! We are aimed to fix all the bugs and keep updating the game with new rooms.

reviews.review_description:
I loved this game, great atmosphere, fun puzzles and creepy story and music.

Can’t wait for more rooms!

Dark Trip is a unique, immersive, and disturbing VR experience that breaks new ground.
Its combination of psychedelic horror, escape puzzles, and visual storytelling makes it a memorable title for those seeking an extraordinary VR adventure. A hidden gem not to be missed!

I have tried numerous times to get this game to work on my Meta 3 to no avail. It starts fine, I see the tv, woman on wheel and I can use the items to spin the wheel n change the blocks but nothing else. The tv is inoperable and I’m guessing that’s how you begin the game. Theres absolutely no direction or instructions on how to play. I have uninstalled it and reinstalled it and it’s the same thing. I’m so disappointed.

developer_response.body:
Dar 4everladyJ, please take our sincere apology for the bug you have encountered. This is a rare bug that sometimes occurs when a user installs the game. It can be fixed on your headset by finding account*.save and deleting it. 

IMPORTANT! When you locate the file please make a copy of it and send it to iworlds.studio@gmail.com or join our Discord at iworlds.studio/discord and share the file in qa channel. We need to investigate the corrupted file to fix this bug once and for all.

- the file is located at sdcard/Android/data/mobi.itales.darktripvr/files
- the simplest thing to locate the file is to download the SideQuest app from https://sidequestvr.com/ and then use its file manager to access your headset files and find the file using the mentioned path.

Once again please take our sincere apology for the situation. Hope you will manage to fix the issues with the instruction provided and will have great time playing the game. In this case please change you review and the score.

reviews.review_title: good game but puzzles a bit confusing
reviews.review_description: i like it, it's a nice game, but it's very confusing. and the use of AI "art" sets off a huge red flag, making the game overall lower quality

developer_response.body: Thank you so much for your feedback! The game is in Early Access, and not everything is quite as planned yet. We're working hard to ensure the release version is flawless :)

reviews.review_description:
Even though the game is currently in Early Access, it already delivers a very enjoyable experience. I did encounter a few minor issues during the initial download, but the developer responded quickly and helped resolve everything without delay — which really shows their dedication and professionalism.

I highly recommend this game to anyone who enjoys escape room–style puzzles and immersive problem-solving experiences. It has great potential, and I’m excited to see how it continues to develop.

Wishing the developers success in completing this project — I’m looking forward to the full release!

developer_response.body:
Dar JokieJay, please take our sincere apology for the bug you have encountered. This is a rare bug that sometimes occurs when a user installs the game. It can be fixed on your headset by finding account*.save and deleting it. 

IMPORTANT! When you locate the file please make a copy of it and send it to iworlds.studio@gmail.com or join our Discord at iworlds.studio/discord and share the file in qa channel. We need to investigate the corrupted file to fix this bug once and for all.

- the file is located at sdcard/Android/data/mobi.itales.darktripvr/files
- the simplest thing to locate the file is to download the SideQuest app from https://sidequestvr.com/ and then use its file manager to access your headset files and find the file using the mentioned path.

Once again please take our sincere apology for the situation. Hope you will manage to fix the issues with the instruction provided and will have great time playing the game. In this case please change you review and the score.

reviews.review_description:
This game is trippy as heck.  The visuals are stunning, the story is thoughtful.  Overall, the experience is impactful, the world peculiar and absurd and a bit unheimlich as well. I am 100% happy
I bought this game and think you will be as well!

Update - accidentally submitted 3
Stars instead of 5 (my bad), updated after response from Dev (thank you!)

developer_response.body: But why only 3 stars? :(

reviews.review_description: Dark Trip did not disappoint.  Creepy, kinky and atmospheric, puzzles are simple enough to solve without frustration while still allowing for a low to moderate level of challenge.  Music, sound effects and graphics all combine for a cohesive and fun escape room experience.

Just finished the game. This is a very great escape room with a twist. Same room, with another different puzzle to solve when you take a pill. 

Still have to get some my pictures.

Get a sneak peak of my gameplay on my YT channel: 

https://youtu.be/23h_OvAokeg?si=xJXhZR1H2SLryU98

developer_response.body: Hey Powergirl! Thank you for the review and for the walkthrough. Why is it unlisted?

reviews.review_description: Really cool to play a game that is more adult oriented. It has a level of kink to it that is fun but could be a lot more (without going too far), and the some of the rooms are really creepy. The game seemed pretty short with an ending that didn’t make a lot sense. The game is enjoyable but could be so much more.

The game is immersive, not too hard to play through, graphics excellent. 
For those who are stuck on the lab screen with the TV not working a quick fix is to go into meta settings, general then storage,find the dark trip app and click on the clear data icon to the right.
It's a fix till the developers have sorted the bug, although it will mean saved game will vanish but it's so easy to play through again once you have finished it before.

I've only been in a few rooms. I really like it. Puzzles are good.. not to difficult not too easy. Ambiance if game is great. Good graphics. I recommend.

Update**
I finished it, thought it was very good... But when will the next part come out?! It left me hangin'

developer_response.body: New rooms are coming. Thank you for your review!

reviews.review_title: Early Access? Wow..Can't Wait For More!
reviews.review_description:
This game feels very well polished. What seemed like bugs I found were just user error (ie I was too close to the wall or was missing something). There are motion options and teleport. The game also allows you to easily change your height 3 levels in active play, by pressing a button. Graphics and themes in each room are very impressive and the puzzles are relatively challenging. People who say they finished it in an hour can't be serious. 12 rooms so far, that would imply it took them 5 mins each.  Absolutely no way unless they were trying to do a speed run after solving it the first time. I can easily spend 10 mins just looking around each room, appreciating the esthetics and designs, gathering items and clues, trying to solve the various puzzles, etc. At a minimum, I would say each room/area will take at least 10-20 mins if you are trying to enjoy the game and not rush through it unnecessarily.

The game doesn't have hints but I honestly don't feel like it needs them. Some of the puzzles are a bit tricky, but just enough to keep the game and narrative interesting. You just have to think through them. Each room has answers if you look around long enough. As you gather photos and items, clues start to form. 

The only issue that I have is that I would like to be able to switch between the drugged mode and normal mode. If you do it too early, you can miss out on key items that may still require analysis or solving. Maybe remove that all together and make it a magic lens like other puzzles have.

The game is very dark, semi sexual, has an interesting premise, and not for children. I love the escape room games genre and this will definitely hold a place in my inventory as it is not just another pathetic knock off of The Room or 7th Guest. Even in it's present form it is worth the buy just to support the team. I honestly feel at the price it's complete but I am truly excited for more.

I like puzzle games and this one was well thought out and just the right difficulty level.
Fully recommend to fans of the puzzle genre.
Very good game, however need more light in some areas so it's actually playable...

developer_response.body: Thank you for your review. We are sorry you had problems with light. Please try your headset's main menu's brightness option. It should work.

reviews.review_description: Genre-wise, this is an escape room puzzle game with a unique mechanic of taking pills that alter your reality, which give clues to the rooms' puzzles. While the puzzles are fun with just the right balance of difficulty, the real draw of the game is how well-implemented the atmosphere is across all the rooms. The game really nails the dark, sci-fi, horror feel. The in-game music that you play from an old-fashioned gramophone in one of the rooms is a fine example of the level of detail that completes the immersion. Highly recommended!
I’ve finished the latest chapter (Ritual Express). So far, the game has been everything i hoped it would be.

This game is a series of 12 escape rooms. It is fairly easy to solve all the secrets. It took me about an hour to finish it. It was fun and relaxing to play. The dark ambience, graphics and music are very well done. I believe this is still at its early access stage and I hope that the developer will add more rooms to it.
Great game !!! I can't wait for more.

reviews.review_title: A Fun Adult Dark Escape Room Game
reviews.review_description:
The responsible adult in me requires me to say this first: this is not for kids under 13. Because of the usage of drugs, the sadistic sexual content, the nudity, the references to the devil and the references of some of the sick experiments of the very real Dr. Josef Mengele in this game, this game is for older teenagers and adults. 

Now to the gameplay. 

The version I got has just added the 13th room, the train station. Each room right now is somewhat easy to solve but gets progressively harder to solve. Sometimes taking the drug makes it easy to solve the puzzle in the room and sometimes made it harder, and presented a different puzzle to solve. I will say that you can go through the game without taking the drug except for in the first room which is the game’s tutorial level where you have to take the drug to open the door and find the third picture in that room.

Each room has 3 pictures in it except for the 13th room, the train station, for now which I expect with change shortly. The catch is that only 2 or 1 pictures can be found in the normal room without using the hallucinatory drug, and the final 1 or 2 pictures can be found only by using the drug. And sometimes, there is a different puzzle in the room to solve when using the drug than the normal room. 

My advice is to play this game through without taking the drug as much as possible and then don’t worry about missing the 3rd picture in each room. Then play it again to collect all the pictures and use the drugs in each room with different puzzles in some. This game does have a replay factor and a second hidden game within it. That is a good thing.

Also the codes and passwords change every time you play it so it is never the same way twice. That is another plus to it.

As a 60 year old woman, loved this game. Challenging and fun.

Hope they make more, I'll buy them.

developer_response.body: Thank you for your kind words!

reviews.review_description:
I am not sure what is going on in this story? Nazi's? Witches? Mediums? Drugs? Bondage? Torture? 
But the puzzles are pretty good and the graphics generally look pretty nice. 
I hope there will be more added to game and story soon!
I had a few issues with room scale mode trying to start me outside of my boundary. I also had a problem completeing the room with the purple light beams as the footprints and the light beam would not line up. I had to quit and restart the level a couple of times.

developer_response.body: Sorry for the bugs you have encountered. We keep fixing them. Please join to iworlds.studio/discord to help us improve and polish the game.

reviews.review_description:
Once you get to grips with the mechanics of the game, the puzzles are good. Just enough for a quick jolt to the grey matter. The game freezing upon playing again after a break is a pain, a start again so,ves this(not hard reset)..!
The darkness of the hand, against the objects does cause some frustration, but you do adapt. I hope it continues further, or another startle of gene would be good, it’s well worth buying it. Would love the save to encompass where you actually are in the level, instead of playing it again. But an enjoyable gam, keep it team..👌💯👌👏🏻👏🏻🥂

Really enjoyed this first episode. I'm mid-60s but managed to get my head around all the puzzles (eventually). A hints/tips (like The Room or 7th Guest) would be nice. I'd also like to be able to  'switch off/on' the hallucinations somehow - so I can see what's been added (again, like the glasses in The Room). Brilliant visuals. I'm now running through it again to find all the photos. One final thing, I can't seem to get out of stationery mode (into room space mode).
Look forward to updates.

developer_response.body: Thank you so much for you review! The "stationery mode / room space mode" switch is in the walkie-talkie that acts as in-game options - please check it again and find it there.

reviews.review_description: This looks like a cool escape room experience, but the smooth motion is making me really queasy.  I see reviews here that mention the ability to teleport instead, but I don't see how to switch to that?

developer_response.body: Hi Kathy, thank you for your feedback. If you during the game you push "menu" button on the left controller - you will see the options menu (it looks like a walkie-talkie in your left hand). There you can choose a motion control method that is suitable for you. If you fell queasy with smooth motion, there are freemovement and teleportation options there. Let us know if this works for you and join our Discord channel at iworlds.studio/discord

reviews.review_description:
I really enjoyed this. Dark and a bit trippy. However, since this is on early access, there is only the 1st episode to play. A couple of hours playtime..

Update 
Needs a hint system. I suck at puzzles. I had to give up on the first room of the mental lab - the part with the lady on the wall with the electricity. No solutions online. Shame, I wanted to see the other rooms.

developer_response.body: Thanks for the review and advice. We will try to add hints or highlight ways to solve puzzles.

reviews.review_description: I have to reload this every time I play as the movement gets stuck and the game fails to progress. Great graphics and puzzles . Strange no one mentioned the heavy Nazi and sadism aspects. It’s disturbing, but I think that’s the point.

For the price this is really good. It’s early access, still in development so I’ll be brutal.  

The puzzles are very easy (and logical) compared to some other games of this kind. Maybe a negative for some people but I think it’s good, it’s not frustrating. I would like the next levels to be harder though. 

It promises a trippy, Hellraiser (the leather, not the gore) type experience but doesn’t quite deliver on that.  I think it’s held back a lot by the Quest and by budget in that regard. 

You can take a pill which makes the rooms change, adds a drugged effect and reveals secrets. This is nice but some of the “trippy” changes are a bit naff looking. Static skulls hanging in chains. Eyeballs on tentacles. That stuff is just “eh”. 

I like the sorta sexual body horror things like stuff changing to have a legs/fishnets type look.    I’d lean into that more organic stuff. 


If we could get somethjng like 7th Guest type ghosts in this thing and some depth /shaders on things it’d be awesome. Some rooms are very sterile and static. 

Towards the end some rooms have a bit more going on visually and do look pretty great. 

I would say that it gets better as it goes on and I was pretty disappointed when it ended as I’d really got into it. Eagerly awaiting the next chapter.
I paid very little for this gem of a game. Worth every penny. Spooky, dark, and downright weird. If you enjoy games like The room, and ghost town, this game, though stranger , is probably for you.

reviews.review_title: Good game for early access status
reviews.review_description: Fun game to play with a unique twist. It's early access I need to go back and see if some of the sticking points have been fixed. Might need to add a little more light for the Quest version it's very dark when trying to find things. I've really enjoyed the gameplay so far.

Just played through the entire game in its current state. I literally stumbled across this by mistake while I was scrolling through games on sale, so I picked it up for just £1.99 which is an absolute steal. I'd say the regular price of £7.99 isn't too bad for what you get. It took me between 1-2 hours to complete in total but I will look out for updates.

Firstly, this is definitely aimed a bit more at mature audiences with some of the darker, twisted imagery and adult themes. Overall, the quality of the escape rooms is reasonably good, with some interesting designs and puzzles. However, the final segment with the train (as it currently stands) felt a bit dull and drab compared with the previous rooms.

I'd say that it's fairly intuitive, but it wasn't till I was about 2 thirds into the game that I realised the notebook updates as you progress to each room so you can read new pages. Each new page gives some backstory, but it may also drop some hints about solving the puzzles in the current room. I wished I'd realised this a bit earlier, but it didn't impede my progress too much.

Another thing to note is that I think you can potentially miss some of the collectables (3 photos to collect in each room) if you don't take the psychedelic pills at the right time or leave it too late to reveal certain things in the room. I had to go back to some of the rooms for photos that I'd missed. Which leads me to my main gripe, which I'm sure can be fixed unless I've missed something. As far as I'm aware, you can't go back and replay individual rooms. Instead, you have to do a reset on the TV in the main options menu and start from the very beginning in the first room. Which is exactly what I did a couple of times. If I'm wrong about this devs, please correct me.

Apart from a few minor points listed above, I think this is a great start, and I look forward to future updates and chapters. I know from some of the previous reviews and replies from the devs that they do take heed and will continue to tweak the game while it's still in the early access stage.

developer_response.body:
Hi, 

Thank you for you review!

> If I'm wrong about this devs, please correct me.

You don't have to reset the TV,  but the current design implies that you complete the whole game more than once if you want to get all the collectibles as some of them can be obtained only in hallucinatory state. And when you get pills in the beginning their number is less than the number of rooms.

P.S. The 13th room will be improved. This is the early version that you have tried)

reviews.review_description:
This is a fantastic, atmospheric puzzle game in the vein of The Room series, but with a much more grown up, Lovecraftian feel to it. Where The Room has lenses that reveal hidden aspects, this has hallucinogenic pills that serve a similar purpose!

I’ve thoroughly enjoyed it up to the point of getting completely stuck in the room with the tree, the two pumps and the 4 screens - I’ve lost hours trying to figure it out to no avail, and there’s no hint system to help you when you get stuck, plus there aren’t any walkthroughs or guides for this particular room online yet, and even AI can’t help me get through it! I think the game would be better if it had a hint system as, for me, when you hit a brick wall figuratively you can’t proceed any further. I would have knocked a star off my review for this omission but this game deserves 5 stars for its excellent atmosphere, theme originality and unique setting. Edit to add - I got through the room now, don’t do drugs kids!

Fantastic puzzler game, recommended to all adults, wouldn’t be letting the kids play it tho til they were older 😂

The problem is YouTube even we don't want we always go there and after see what to do when we are loose in the game but is that after we only stay see how is the puzzle and don't try to find for ourselves, but is an excellent game

This is more a creepy game than a horror game, which is good for me - I have a very low threshold for horror. There are no jump scares. The visuals are good, there are some good puzzles, and a lot of WTF moments.
So far really enjoyed the puzzles and different twists and turns.

I am running in Stationary mode and when I go to the second level (the cabin), finding the hook is next to impossible (I know it is there because of some walk throughs) to find.  I have tried increasing the Q3 brightness and contrast, but no luck - It is like walking around in the dark.  
I did make it into the cabin, but the same thing, even with the lamp lit, you can only see the table and some of the valves on the wall.  No luck with any details of the generator.
There needs to be a brightness control within the game as well as possibly a contrast control. 
For me, this game is UNPLAYABLE.


UPDATE: 
With some support from the developer, we determined it was an issue with my Q3.  Apparently, as i was having problems seeing anything in the game, I turned up the contrast.  Which was the wrong thing to do.  
After turning down the Q3 contrast, I am now able to see the scenes and the details.
I am looking forward to continuing my saga through Dark Trip.  And thanks to the developer for the support.

developer_response.body:
Hi,

We are sorry to hear you are experiencing troubles with lighting in our game. The lighting is a part of the game’s setting and some of the puzzles, but the situation you are describing is not normal.

Usually this is resolved by changing the contrast settings on your Quest headset. You can change the settings by pressing the Meta button on the right controller, navigate to Quick controls, then Settings, and Accessibility. From there, select Vision and adjust the Contrast slider to customize the display to your preference. Turning this down to the minimum should help you navigate that room a lot better.

If it doesn’t work for you - please join our discord channel at https://discord.gg/h5xnewgMXE and provide additional details to us - so we can find the ways to fix the issue.

Best,
Alex

reviews.review_description: It's pretty twisted but well done, I'll probably not finish for the same reason I quit watching American horror story. It says 17 and up, I'd keep it away from a 17 year old
This game was awesome! Some very clever if not weird puzzles but very enjoyable. Great graphics and sounds effects. Pity the last room was a bit short but looking forward to more!
Gorgeous gruesome graphics, great story line! Can't wait for more!

The graphics are excellent and that helps create a creepy atmosphere in line with the dark theme of the game. Add in the use of pills to progress and it could be contentious to some. I thought the puzzles were some of the best out there and all are quite unique. I took well over an hour to make it through.
It is still in early access and does end abruptly at the moment.

Had a slight controller issue that corrected after a fresh restart. Puzzles are fairly straightforward. Taking the pills are a little freaky and trippy. If you’re after something different with reasonable graphics this game is worth a shot.

developer_response.body: Can you please join out Discord https://discord.gg/h5xnewgMXE and provide us a little more details about the bug you have encountered?

reviews.review_title: Too many issues in current state
reviews.review_description:
Looks interesting but unfortunately has too many issues - hopefully will improve- Controls are not intuitive and can’t accomplish basic tasks like grabbing items I would expect to be able to grab.  
The only way to see anything is to turn headset contrast all the way down and brightness all the way up which isn’t ideal to have to adjust at the headset level.  The psych double vision effects are overdone and not enjoyable but cannot be disabled.  No hints options and not many adjustable settings. Overall while this may be forgiven as a free beta it’s not ok in a paid app even if early access.  (Will try again in a few months and update if any significant changes)

developer_response.body:
Thank you for you feedback eagleloc!

Can you please join our discord channel https://discord.gg/h5xnewgMXE? We will be glad to discuss your complains in person and find out exact ways to improve the game in such a way that will resolve your feedback.

Best,
Alex

reviews.review_description: Finished it with no hints, obviously (it would help a bit, though). So when will we see the finished product? The psychedelic drugs seemed to become less and less needed as the game progressed or at least near the "end". The lore/storyline seems to not be connected to the gameplay? Or am I just being obtuse? Anyway, I love what you're doing. Let's go see the rest of this cool game!

reviews.review_title: This game just keeps getting better
reviews.review_description: The graphics are really impressive. It is a bit on the dark side, but if you like puzzles and escape rooms, this games for you. I have enjoyed it so far and I looking forward to the release of the next room.

reviews.review_title: Like no other, love it.Creepy & Puzzling
reviews.review_description: It is creepy as hell which I love, great graphics. This escape room has already had additional content added since I bought it. If you’re a fan of escape rooms or creepy VR experiences I highly recommend it!

The trailer for Dark Trip caught my attention instantly and the game became a must-play. Welcome to a psychedelic VR experience like no other. My favorite VR game genre is horror puzzle. But Dark Trip isn't scary nor does it have any jump scares. It's dark and it's trippy. Dark Trip is more like a creepy and edgy escape room puzzle game with a twist. Your mission is to search for clues, solve the puzzles, and escape. But you have the option to ingest psychedelic drugs to enhance the experience and view the rooms from a different perspective. It is possible to solve the puzzles without the psychedelic drugs but what fun is that. But there aren't enough psychedelic pills to experience each room while high so you would have to play the game twice to get the full experience. Bonus points for replayability. The puzzles aren't overly complicated to solve but will get your brain gears turning and eyeballs wandering. The game has teleporting and smooth locomotion. You can play stationary while standing or seated or in roomscale if you have a big enough space. There are currently 12 rooms to solve and escape with more in the works from the developers. Check out my review and walkthroughs on YT @MissChiefVR.

reviews.review_title: Good game but too hard to grab anything
reviews.review_description: I really want to like this game but even after playing with my boundary and raising it up it’s still nearly impossible to grab things. They are either too high or too low. Then every time I want to go on a different app I have to change my boundary settings again. Played for about 45 minutes and ended up in a completely dark room in the house and stuck there. Might be returning this unless some of these features get fixed like equipping an auto-grab button by pointing at an object or something similar to that. Hope it gets fixed and will change my review.

developer_response.body:
Hi Vegas Turtle,

We are sorry to hear you are experiencing troubles in our game. May be these tips will help you:

1. High settings. There is a screen with the height setting in the options menu (walkie-talkie). You can adjust the basic height there and then when playing the game you can force your character to crawl and stand up using buttons on the left controller. These tools were added a few months ago after we had similar complaints as yours and they are designed to make your gameplay experience convenient. Please check them and let us know if this resolves your troubles.

2. In the 3rd room the complete darkness is a part of the puzzle. Simply grab the matches you see instantly and use them to lighten a lamp nearby. 

Let us know if our tips help.

P.S. If you need any additional support - please join our discord at https://discord.gg/h5xnewgMXE

reviews.review_description:
Interesting dark puzzles. Really enjoyed doing this game. The creator of this has a dark mind. 
Yes the idea of procreation is like that. XX

I love story-based escape room type games, and this game is now one of my favourites. Can’t remember which level, but I honestly thought it had some horror cause of how eerie the environment was. Got stuck a couple times, but pushed through. Had to give this 5 stars, amazing.

Just finished the early release of Dark Trip and absolutely loved it! A dark, puzzling escape room game, it is way into the weird, which is where I like to be. Can’t wait for the rest of this to come out. Much kudos to the devs! This was super fun with really trippy graphics and provided a great deal of enjoyment.
Its not too much but it IS early access. Its an amazing game with lots of potential. I cant wait to see what's in store for the future.😁

reviews.review_title: great, dark, disturbing: one of the best
reviews.review_description:
One of the best escape room games. And one of the best looking ones, too.  If not THE best looking.
Definetly the weirdest and most disturbing one. 
Love it!

reviews.review_title: Trippy escape room game gorgeous art!
reviews.review_description: This game is a work of art...it's also an escape room/puzzle kinda thing... but what sets it apart from the others is if you chose to take the pills the whole room transforms, the effects of tripping in VR are just incredible. The puzzles I've encountered so far aren't too hard it's just finding things, I had to cheat to find the hook in the first room but if I wasn't short of time I think I'd have found it eventually.
Like the game. It’s a nice escape room puzzle game but do not like the drug taking part. Maybe you can find another alternative. That’s why I am giving it a 4 star.

reviews.review_title: Must Play Escape Room - Super Immersive!
reviews.review_description:
Dark Trip is a very eerie, bizarre, and super immersive VR escape room game. Its truly unique design, perfectly crafted environments, and complementary sound effects and music transform it into something special. It features smart, challenging puzzles and an exciting narrative.

The game skillfully uses a combination of imagery, videos, visual effects, and sound to deliver an unsettling, eerie atmosphere that carries the player throughout. 

One of the most immersive escape room games I've played in VR, can't wait for more content - Well done devs!
Pretty nice Puzzle Game with awesome and creative Environments. Too sad it only took me a bit over an Hour to finish this. More Chapters are coming, i just hope i don't have to wait too long.

Looks great, sounds are immersive, plays well, nice puzzles: they will tax you just a tiny bit but aren't ridiculous and are all very fun. It would be a very good creepy escape room game as is, but the psychotropic substance use twist elevates the experience. Full locomotion (if you want it), adjustable height and several other options, all intuitive to use, as are in-game controls. There is no physics engine here, but it doesn't really need one. A darker, creepier, simpler "The 7th guest" but with drugs. Love it.

Spooky is awesome can teleport or smooth which is amazing love the clues in beginning of game .The puzzles in this escape room genre are well-designed, featuring challenges that typically involve collecting and utilizing items, operating machinery, and deciphering clues - conventional elements elevated by innovative reality-shifting mechanics. I would give it 5 stars ⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️
..for now.

If you enjoy horror and escape room puzzles this one hits the spot. slightly psychedelic  in its looks and feel had me hooked from the beginning, lots of interactive things do and find. great atmospherics. Definitely one for the library.
This game is amazing, all the Rooms are really enjoyable. 11 in all . They are  just right, not to difficult but you do have to think. Realy looking forward to episode 2
Whoever made this game  has a bright future in this field i took off my headset multiple times because I thought someone was in the room with me 👍🏿👍🏿👍🏿👍🏿👍🏿👍🏿

This is a really great game, and it is only Episode 1 out of 3 planned (this is early access). First of all, though it may look a little scary, and it has a bit of a Silent Hill vibe, it is really a "friendly" or "soft-core" kind of horror. Hard to articulate. There aren't any jump scares, but you might get startled when you turn around from time to time.

The art is first-rate and unique. Part of the joy of this game is just enjoying the art work. The puzzles are fun,  fairly straight-forward and you are unlikely to need hints or a walk-through.

Each room can be solved either "normally" or "while hallucinating." Some of the puzzles overlap in each mode, but some are also unique to each mode. There's also a photo album of collectible photos, 30 in all (for Ep 1). So, replay value is pretty decent.

I highly recommend this game! I assume they will raise the price when all 3 episodes are available and it comes out of early release, so do yourself a favor and get it now. Even now the polish level is high, so don't worry about early release meaning buggy or poor quality, for the most part everything that is there now is at the level you'd expect for a fully released game.
The imagination and creep factor is worth it! The gameplay is smooth and I had no technical issues. If you’re thinking of getting it - go for it!
This is an interesting game and so far it's  quite enjoyable. The big problem i have is it is so dark i can't see everything. I have to watch parts of it on utube and guess where the item is .

developer_response.body: Hi! Thank you so much for your review. What you describe is a common problem for some of the Meta devices. Just use Main Settings menu of your device and try changing brightness level. It should work for you. Please let us know if it does.

reviews.review_description: A short but solid indie escape room. Simple graphics, but good and interesting puzzles.

This is the weird creepy VR game I've always wanted. Horror, but without jump scares. A creepy, nightmarish world full of crazy. In all the best ways. It says Early Access, but it's already way worth the purchase and better than most VR games on this store. And this Dev is doing Early Access right, with regular updates and a discord channel and ponies and cake.

This is an absolutely amazing puzzle escape room game. It’s like the puzzle solving of “The Room,” but with the weirdness of Otherworld/Meow Wolf, and the dark/demented aspects of Hellraiser. If you like any of those things, this is a must buy; it is definitely a “Dark Trip.”

I can’t wait for the new levels to be released. 

Great work, devs!

If anyone is umming and arring about whether to get this, just get it.. you won’t be disappointed!  
Loved it .. for an early release it’s been faultless..  :)

reviews.review_title: Some Truly Great Rooms to Escape!
reviews.review_description:
The game seems to be coming together in great ways over the months since it initially released, with the controls now being made much more bearable and even a full extra room at the very beginning of the game that wasn't there the last time I completed it. I can't wait to see what more the developers can do with it and for more "Chapters", but for now, I'll just keep eagerly awaiting! I'll actually go through the game yet again whilst I wait. 😅

I definitely recommend this great atmospheric puzzler!

reviews.review_title: THE BEST and MOST UNIQUE YET!
reviews.review_description: This game has been the most enjoyable and innovative escape room to date!! exactly what I hav wanted out of an escape room!! It’s like The Room but so much better 👏🏼Honestly only thing I would change is that it wouldnt hav never ended!! When I say can’t wait for mor!!!YA’LL  best get this game and why it’s this price! I feel like I stole from them after playing it basically!! I loathe teleporting but the way they hav it even is different then any game i hav played in VR!! Y’all, I hav 786 games and I loath teleporting entirely!! No problem on this one!!The overall experience is outstanding. These developers clearly put their heart and soul into this project, and it shows!! 10/10!!🙌🏼🫶🏻

developer_response.body: O my! Thank you so much for this emotional review!

reviews.review_description: I love this game. It’s such a unique puzzle game. Episode 1 is short but for the price you can’t go wrong especially since they are adding more episodes at a later date!! Can’t wait.
